Not directly relevant. I have sufficient domain knowledge from the amyloid master equation literature (Cohen, Meisl, Knowles). Let me rely on established structure.

The key physics for Aβ42 aggregation (Knowles/Cohen master equation, secondary-nucleation dominated):
- dM/dt (fibril mass) has the sigmoidal form driven by elongation (∝ free monomer × fibril mass) and secondary nucleation (∝ monomer^n2 × fibril mass).
- The classic self-consistent solution: dc/dt ∝ (plateau−c)·[primary + secondary·c], where secondary is the autocatalytic term.

The best family (0.9572) uses `capacity*(source + secondary)` with a surface-saturation rollover. The current parent uses a Gompertz log kernel (0.9483).
